mapping.a_element: Gross-Pitaevskii equation with an attractive delta function potential
mapping.b_element: nonlinear Schrödinger equation with a spatially adiabatic external potential

mapping.a_element: incident soliton split into reflected and transmitted components
high velocity limit governing soliton splitting
mapping.b_element: high initial relative speed of solitons governing dynamics

mapping.a_element: explicit analytic formulas for reflected and transmitted portions
mapping.b_element: dynamics of centers of mass approximately determined by external potential
error terms due to radiation damping and extended nature of solitons

brief_justification: While both papers study soliton dynamics under perturbations (delta potential vs. external potential and collision), paper A focuses on scattering and splitting due to a localized impurity with explicit transmission/reflection formulas, whereas paper B examines soliton-soliton collisions and their post-collision shape preservation, governed by adiabatic potential effects and radiation damping. The core mechanisms (scattering off a delta impurity vs. soliton-soliton interaction with external potential) differ fundamentally.
